mnemonics: CREATE TABLE EMPLOYEE (EMPNO INT, ENAME VARCHAR(20), JOB VARCHAR(10), MANAGER_NO INT, SAL DECIMAL(10, 2), COMMISSION DECIMAL(10, 2))
INSERT INTO EMPLOYEE (EMPNO, ENAME, JOB, MANAGER_NO, SAL, COMMISSION) VALUES (1, 'John Doe', 'Manager', 123, 50000.00, 5000.00)
INSERT INTO EMPLOYEE (EMPNO, ENAME, JOB, MANAGER_NO, SAL, COMMISSION) VALUES (2, 'Jane Smith', 'Employee', NULL, 40000.00, 2000.00)
keyPoints: Use CREATE TABLE to define the structure of the employee table.
Use INSERT INTO to insert data into the employee table.
Use COMMIT to persist the changes to the database.
Use ROLLBACK to undo the changes made to the database in case of an error.
